verdict.headline: Safe to transact — sanctions screens are clean and the wallet shows a healthy on-chain balance with no negative signals.
verdict.reasoning: Both the Chainalysis on-chain oracle and the x402 sanctions service returned clean (not sanctioned), which is the strongest deterministic signal available. The address has no known label (positive or negative) and no adverse web-sentiment hits, while on-chain history shows a substantial ~11.08 ETH balance, suggesting a real, funded wallet rather than a throwaway. ENS is not set, which is neutral. With sanctions clean and no negative indicators across labels, history, or sentiment, the wallet is safe to transact with at medium confidence (lack of an identifying label prevents a high-confidence positive).

findings.finding: Chainalysis on-chain sanctions oracle returned isSanctioned=false and x402 sanctions service reported no matches against active sanctions lists.
No known label or entity type returned; no risk keywords (scam, mixer, phisher, etc.) present. Neutral signal.
Wallet holds ~11.08 ETH, indicating a funded, active address — positive supporting evidence.
No adverse web-sentiment hits referencing scam, hack, exploit, or lawsuit.
No ENS reverse record set (ensName=null); neutral signal.

Resource 1 is the only labels candidate with a confirmed 0% failure rate, which is the strongest signal; its description covers known-entity labeling (exchange, mixer, contract) at a low price despite 1/3 completeness.

Resource 1 is the only onchain_history candidate combining a confirmed 0% failure rate with inputInfoCompleteness of 2/3, making it clearly superior to all others which are either untested or less complete.
